title: Bob Wiacek
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
{{Otheruses|Bob}}
'''Bob Wiacek''' is a comic book inker who worked on the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series. He alternated inking chores with [[Gene Day]] after [[Terry Austin]] left the series in 1978 and continued regularly until the [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] adaptation, and he also co-created the characters of [[Remel Fud]], [[Dafi (bounty hunter)|Dafi]] and [[Slssk]]. He has worked much of his career for [[Marvel Comics]] contributing to issues of several titles through the 1980s and 1990s including: [[Wikipedia:Alpha Flight|''Alpha Flight'']], [[Wikipedia:Avengers (comics)|''Avengers'']], [[Wikipedia:Uncanny X-Men|''Uncanny X-Men'']], ''Coyote'', ''Damage Control'', [[Wikipedia:Excalibur (comics)|''Excalibur'']], [[Wikipedia:Fantastic Four|''Fantastic Four'']], [[Wikipedia:Iron Man|''Iron Man'']], [[Wikipedia:Silver Surfer|''Silver Surfer'']] and [[Wikipedia:X-Factor|''X-Factor'']].
